purpose: The heart of the yolo CLI: `yolo run` (also the default `yolo -- cmd` path) assembles the full podman / Apple Container argv — mounts, env vars, networking, GPU/KVM/devices, host services — then launches a fresh jail or execs into an existing one via the tty proxy. runtime.py owns runtime detection (podman vs Apple `container` vs macos-user), deterministic container naming, and container lookup/removal/inspection wrappers. network.py is the host half of forward_host_ports: socat UNIX-LISTEN→TCP tunnels whose sockets are bind-mounted into the jail.

files.role: run()/ps()/doctor() Typer commands; container argv assembly (3147 lines); repo-root resolution; workspace-readonly + venv-shadow + scratch mount builders; entrypoint preflight dry-run; agent YOLO-flag injection; orphan-jail reaping via owner-PID files; embedded bash provisioning script

Runtime detection/selection (_runtime, _runtime_for_check, _is_apple_container, _runtime_is_connectable); container_name_for_workspace; find_running/existing_container; stale removal; tracking files; cgroup resolution; stuck-container heuristic; Podman Machine memory advisory
Host-side socat port forwarding: _parse_port_forwards, start_host_port_forwarding, cleanup_port_forwarding

public_surface.name: yolo run / yolo -- <cmd>
public_surface.detail: Options: --network bridge|host (default bridge), --new (force fresh container), --profile (timing report), --dry-run (macos-user runtime only, else exit 1). Extra args (allow_extra_args + ignore_unknown_options) become the in-jail command; empty → bash. Leading binary matching an agent registry bin gets yolo flags auto-injected at index 1 (gemini→--yolo, copilot→--yolo --no-auto-update, claude→--dangerously-skip-permissions; skipped if flag or alias e.g. -y already present).
public_surface.consumers: end users; registered in cli/__init__.py app.command()

public_surface.name: yolo ps
public_surface.detail: Lists yolo-* containers as a fixed-width table CONTAINER/STATUS/WORKSPACE; podman: `ps --filter name=^yolo- --format {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.RunningFor}}`; AC: parses `container ls` table skipping header. Prints 'No running jails.' and prunes ALL tracking files when empty; prunes stale tracking files otherwise; appends '⚠ N problem jail(s)' block (workspace gone / stuck-in-provisioning via `<rt> top <name> -eo comm`).

public_surface.name: yolo doctor [--build/--no-build]
public_surface.detail: Alias delegating to check_cmd.check(build=...).

public_surface.name: YOLO_RUNTIME (read)
public_surface.detail: Overrides runtime selection if in ALL_RUNTIMES=('podman','container','macos-user'). Auto-detect order: macOS container→podman (binary must pass _is_apple_container version-banner check AND daemon connectable — `container system status` contains 'running' / `<rt> info` rc=0); Linux podman only. No runtime → red error + exit 1.
public_surface.consumers: runtime.py; users; nested jails (set to podman by run_cmd)

public_surface.name: YOLO_REPO_ROOT (read)
public_surface.detail: Repo-root resolution step 1; only honored if flake.nix or src/entrypoint/__init__.py exists under it. Fallbacks: source-checkout detection via cli/__init__.py path parents; installed-wheel staging into ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/nix-build-root (mtime-gated repopulate; rename-aside to nix-build-root.old.<uuid>, never rmtree — live podman binds pin inodes); user config repo_path; else exit 1 with fix hint.

public_surface.name: YOLO_VERSION (read as in-jail sentinel)
public_surface.detail: Presence means 'running inside a jail': suppresses _retire_jail_made_venv and the YOLO_STORE_PRUNE_OK grant + relay orphan reaping.

public_surface.name: YOLO_DEBUG (read)
public_surface.detail: If set, prints the full shlex-quoted container run argv to stderr before launch — the natural golden-output parity hook.

public_surface.name: YOLO_NIX_HOST_DAEMON (read)
public_surface.detail: Opt-in on macOS to mount host /nix daemon socket + /nix/store:ro + NIX_REMOTE=daemon (gated by _should_mount_host_nix; automatic on Linux when both paths exist and runtime != container).

public_surface.name: TERM (read→forwarded)
public_surface.detail: Forwarded verbatim as -e TERM=... when present.

public_surface.name: container env block (-e contract)
public_surface.detail: Fixed env set the entrypoint depends on: JAIL_HOME=/home/agent, NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=/home/agent/.npm-global, NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/agent/.cache/npm, GOPATH=/home/agent/go, MISE_DATA_DIR=/mise, MISE_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/mise-cache, MISE_PYTHON_PRECOMPILED_FLAVOR=install_only, MISE_PYTHON_GITHUB_ATTESTATIONS=false, MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS=/workspace, MISE_ENV=jail, RUSTUP_HOME=/mise/rustup, CARGO_HOME=/mise/cargo, MISE_YES=1, COPILOT_ALLOW_ALL=true, IS_SANDBOX=1, LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/lib:/usr/lib:/usr/lib/<multilib>, HOME=/home/agent, EDITOR=cat, VISUAL=nvim, PI_TELEMETRY=0, PAGER=cat, GIT_PAGER=cat, YOLO_BLOCK_CONFIG=<json>, TZ=<host tz, optional>, YOLO_HOST_DIR=<workspace>, YOLO_VERSION=<git describe|unknown>, OVERMIND_SOCKET=/tmp/overmind.sock, YOLO_MISE_TOOLS/<LSP_SERVERS>/<LSP_NPM_INSTALL newline-sep>/<LSP_GO_INSTALL>/<MCP_SERVERS>/<MCP_PRESETS>/<AGENTS> all JSON, YOLO_RUNTIME=podman (always), YOLO_REPO_ROOT=/opt/yolo-jail, MISE_DISABLE_TOOLS=<merged>.

public_surface.name: conditional container env vars
public_surface.detail: YOLO_GIT_NAME/YOLO_GIT_EMAIL (from `git config --get user.name/email`), YOLO_JJ_NAME/YOLO_JJ_EMAIL (from `jj config get`, quotes stripped) — also on the exec/attach path; YOLO_GLOBAL_GITIGNORE=/home/agent/.config/git/ignore; YOLO_PUBLISHED_PORTS=<json '["port/proto",...]'> (podman+publish only); YOLO_FORWARD_HOST_PORTS=<json>; YOLO_FWD_HOST_GATEWAY=host.containers.internal (macOS podman); YOLO_HOST_CLAUDE_FILES=<json list>; YOLO_STORE_PRUNE_OK=1 (only when host CLI proves zero other live jails); YOLO_PROFILE=1; NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES / NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES; ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES / HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES (only when devices != 'all'); HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION; LIBVA_DRIVERS_PATH=/lib/dri:/usr/lib/dri; NIX_REMOTE=daemon; TERM; YOLO_SERVICE_<NAME>_SOCKET per host service (inserted just before image arg).

public_surface.name: podman run argv contract (fresh launch)
public_surface.detail: podman run --rm -i --init --cgroupns=private --read-only --name <cname> [--read-only-tmpfs=false Linux-only] --pull=never --log-driver none --security-opt unmask=/proc/sys [-t if stdout tty] + mounts + env + --workdir /workspace ... <image=localhost/yolo-jail:latest> yolo-entrypoint '<final_internal_cmd bash script>'. Userns branches: in-container(nested): --security-opt label=disable --userns host --cap-add SYS_ADMIN,MKNOD,NET_ADMIN,NET_RAW + --device /dev/fuse,/dev/net/tun if present; NVIDIA GPU: label=disable --uidmap 0:0:1 --uidmap 1:1:65536 --gidmap 0:0:1 --gidmap 1:1:65536 --runtime runc --cap-add SYS_ADMIN,NET_ADMIN,NET_RAW; default host: label=disable --device /dev/fuse --uidmap/--gidmap identity pairs --cap-add SYS_ADMIN,MKNOD,NET_ADMIN,NET_RAW + /dev/net/tun if exists. Net: nested→--net=host, non-bridge→--net=<mode>, bridge→no flag (pasta default). --pids-limit 32768 default (config override); --memory/--cpus only if configured (AC gets defaults half-host, min 4g/2cpu). GPU: --ulimit memlock=<host hard cap or -1:-1>, --device nvidia.com/gpu=all|N or amd.com/gpu=... or /dev/kfd+/dev/dri, AMD adds --group-add keep-groups; kvm: --device /dev/kvm + keep-groups; publish: -p <spec> + --sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.route_localnet=1.
public_surface.consumers: podman/crun/runc; parity-test golden surface via YOLO_DEBUG

public_surface.name: podman mount contract (fresh launch, ordered)
public_surface.detail: -v <workspace>:/workspace; GLOBAL_HOME:/home/agent:ro; per-workspace overlays from <ws>/.yolo/home: npm-global, local, go, yolo-shims, config → /home/agent/.<same>; GLOBAL_CACHE:/home/agent/.cache; file overlays yolo-bootstrap.sh, yolo-venv-precreate.sh, yolo-perf.log, yolo-socat.log, yolo-entrypoint.lock, yolo-ca-bundle.crt, yolo-installed-lsps → /home/agent/.<name>; bash_history; ssh dir (0700); mise store <GLOBAL>/mise-or named volume yolo-mise-data-v2 (macOS) → /mise; scratch (_scratch_mount_args: volume mode = anonymous -v /tmp,/var/tmp,/var/lib/containers,/var/cache/containers + --tmpfs /run + --tmpfs /dev/shm:size=2g; tmpfs mode = --tmpfs with exec,mode=1777); per-selected-agent overlay dirs (spec.overlay_dirs) → /home/agent/.<dir>; claude → GLOBAL_HOME/.claude-shared-credentials rw; yolo-user-env.sh → /home/agent/.config/yolo-user-env.sh; repo → /opt/yolo-jail:ro (workspace itself if it IS a yolo source tree); nix socket/store; git global ignore file:ro (deref if bind-mountpoint via ws_state copy); publish/mounts (:ro /ctx/... — SKIPPED on AC with warning since :ro is ignored, apple/container#889); host-services dir → /run/yolo-services:rw; port-forward dir → /tmp/yolo-fwd:rw; ~/.config/nvim → /ctx/host-nvim-config:ro; /dev/null shadows over /workspace/.vscode/mcp.json and /workspace/.overmind.sock; workspace_readonly overlays (+ yolo-jail.jsonc:ro lock); venv shadow mounts (ws_state/venv-shadows/<rel with / → __> → /workspace/<rel>); user config → /home/agent/.config/yolo-jail/config.jsonc:ro; skills staging per agent :ro; briefing files (AGENTS-*.md/CLAUDE.md) :ro; host-claude files → /ctx/host-claude/<f>:ro. AC variant: ws_state as single writable /home/agent (~22 virtiofs device limit), all --tmpfs bare paths, single-file mounts materialized by copy into ws_state (_ac_materialize_under_ws_state), --publish-socket <host.sock>:/tmp/yolo-fwd/port-N.sock.
public_surface.consumers: container runtime; entrypoint expects these exact in-jail paths

public_surface.name: exec/attach argv contract
public_surface.detail: <runtime> exec -i [-t if stdout tty] [-e YOLO_GIT_NAME=... etc] <cname> yolo-entrypoint '<shlex.join(cmd)>' — used when find_running_container hits (and on the post-lock race re-check). Wrapped in run_with_proxy; exit code propagated via sys.exit(rc).

public_surface.name: final_internal_cmd embedded bash script
public_surface.detail: Byte-exact bash passed as last argv element after 'yolo-entrypoint': provisioning header printf → setup_script (YOLO_BYPASS_SHIMS=1 sh -c 'mise trust --all --quiet; conditional dangling-symlink store prune when YOLO_STORE_PRUNE_OK=1; mise install --quiet; mise upgrade --yes with grep -v "^mise WARN" filter preserving real rc; ~/.yolo-bootstrap.sh; ~/.yolo-venv-precreate.sh') teed into /workspace/.yolo/startup.log with PIPESTATUS[0] rc capture; on failure writes 'PROVISIONING FAILED (exit N)' to log, red banner, interactive [Y/n] continue prompt (tty only; YOLO_PROVISION_PROMPT=0 bypasses; n → exit rc); then mise_activate ('. $HOME/.config/yolo-user-env.sh; eval "$(mise env -s bash)"; PATH=$HOME/.yolo-shims:$PATH'); '⚡ Executing: <cmd>' banner; target_cmd. --profile variant adds nanosecond timing instrumentation + perf-log awk extraction + node shim comparison on fd 3.

public_surface.name: ~/.config/yolo-user-env.sh (via ws_state/yolo-user-env.sh)
public_surface.detail: Generated sourceable sh: two comment lines then `export KEY=${KEY:-'val'}` per env_sources var, single-quote-escaped ('\''). Launch-env-wins precedence mirrored by entrypoint._hydrate_env_from_user_env_file — must stay in sync. Always exists (touched empty).

public_surface.name: <workspace>/.yolo/home (ws_state)
public_surface.detail: Per-workspace overlay tree created host-side: subdirs npm-global,local,go,yolo-shims,config,ssh(0700),<agent overlay dirs>,venv-shadows/; touched files bash_history, yolo-bootstrap.sh, yolo-venv-precreate.sh, yolo-perf.log, yolo-socat.log, yolo-entrypoint.lock, yolo-ca-bundle.crt, yolo-installed-lsps, yolo-user-env.sh; deref copies for nested-jail single-file mounts (.config/git/ignore, .config/yolo-jail/*, ctx-host-claude/*). Agent auth seeded from GLOBAL_HOME (_seed_agent_dir), claude.json two-way sync, legacy overlay migrations (claude-projects→claude/projects etc.).

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/containers/<cname>
public_surface.detail: Tracking file: absolute workspace path + trailing newline (write_container_tracking). Read by _get_container_workspace/ps; removed by cleanup_container_tracking / stale removal.

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/owners/<cname>
public_surface.detail: Owner-PID file: '<pid>\n' of the yolo run that started the jail. _reap_orphaned_jails on every run: for each live yolo-* container with a recorded dead owner PID (os.kill(pid,0) semantics: ESRCH=dead, EPERM=alive, other=assume alive) → `podman stop -t 5` (AC: `container stop`, 30s) + file removal. No file → never reaped.

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/locks/<cname>.lock
public_surface.detail: Workspace launch lock: open + fcntl.flock LOCK_EX (blocking); flock failure only warns. Post-acquire re-check attaches if raced. Released by on_started callback after polling find_running_container 20×0.25s, or on error paths.

public_surface.name: /tmp/yolo-fwd-<cname>/port-<port>.sock (macOS: /tmp resolved to /private/tmp)
public_surface.detail: Host-side socat sockets, one per forward_host_ports entry; dir bind-mounted at /tmp/yolo-fwd in-jail (Linux) or bridged via --publish-socket (AC). socat argv: `socat UNIX-LISTEN:<sock>,fork,mode=777 TCP:127.0.0.1:<host_port>`; stderr → ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/logs/<cname>-socat.log (append). Startup waits ≤2s at 5ms poll for socket files. Cleanup: terminate(2s)→kill, rmtree socket dir.

public_surface.name: yolo-jail.jsonc keys consumed by run
public_surface.detail: runtime, network.mode/ports/forward_host_ports, security (blocked tools), mounts ('host[:container]' — last-colon-before-/ split, default /ctx/<basename>), workspace_readonly, per_side_paths, ephemeral_storage ('volume'|'tmpfs'), devices (str path | {usb: 'vid:pid', description} | {cgroup_rule}), gpu.{enabled,vendor(nvidia|amd),devices,capabilities,mode(devices|cdi),hsa_override_gfx_version,seccomp_unconfined,vaapi}, kvm, resources.{memory,cpus,pids_limit}, lsp_servers (recipes: python→pyright npm, typescript→typescript-language-server+typescript, go→gopls; any LSP adds github.com/isaacphi/mcp-language-server@latest go pkg), mcp_servers, mcp_presets, host_claude_files, agents, loopholes, env sources, packages, mise tools, agents_md_extra, repo_path (user config).

public_surface.name: container naming scheme
public_surface.detail: yolo-<sanitized dirname lowercase [^a-z0-9-]→'-' strip('-') [:40], fallback 'jail'>-<sha256(resolved path)[:8]>. Deterministic; the join key for tracking files, owner PIDs, locks, socket dirs, skills staging (AGENTS_DIR/<cname>).

public_surface.name: run exit codes
public_surface.detail: Propagates container/exec rc via sys.exit(rc). Exit 1: config load/validate errors, --dry-run off macos-user, _check_config_changes declined, auto_load_image failure, runtime binary FileNotFoundError. Exit 137 on macOS+podman triggers advisory OOM hint if Podman Machine memory < 4096MB floor.

public_surface.name: SIGHUP/SIGTERM teardown (via tty_proxy callbacks)
public_surface.detail: run supplies on_terminate to run_with_proxy: _stop_jail (podman stop -t 5, timeout+5), cleanup_port_forwarding, lock close, stop_loopholes. Normal exit path does the same minus stop. SIGKILL/host-crash covered by owner-PID reaping on next run. ^Z handled by proxy (out of scope here).

public_surface.name: runtime-CLI probe/inspect invocations
public_surface.detail: Contracts with podman/AC CLIs: `<rt> ps -q --filter name=^/<name>$` (running), `ps -a -q` (existing), `ps -a --format '{{.Names}} {{.State}}'` (_live_yolo_containers, states running/paused/restarting), `<rt> rm <name>` / `container rm --force`, `container ls [--all]` whitespace-table parse skip header, `container system status`, `<rt> info`, `<rt> top <name> -eo comm` (stuck heuristic: all procs in {uv,mise,pip,npm}+infra set), `<rt> inspect --format {{.State.CgroupPath}}/{{.State.Pid}}/{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}`, `container inspect` JSON (.config.env list), `podman machine inspect` JSON ([].Resources.Memory, .State, .Name).

public_surface.name: host subprocess probes
public_surface.detail: git config --get user.name/user.email; git config --global --get core.excludesFile (fallback ~/.config/git/ignore); jj config get user.name/user.email; lsusb -d <vid:pid> (parse 'Bus 001 Device 004:' → /dev/bus/usb/BBB/DDD); sysctl -n hw.memsize (macOS AC memory default); sys.executable -c <entrypoint dry-run code> for preflight.

public_surface.name: /workspace/.yolo/startup.log (in-jail)
public_surface.detail: Provision log: '=== yolo provisioning <ISO8601> ===' header, teed provisioning output, 'PROVISIONING FAILED (exit N)' marker on failure. Fresh per container start.

public_surface.name: YOLO_PROVISION_PROMPT (in-jail, read by generated script)
public_surface.detail: '0' disables the interactive continue/abort prompt after provisioning failure; default continue on Enter, n aborts with provisioning rc.

public_surface.name: ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/nix-build-root[.old.<uuid>]
public_surface.detail: Staged repo copy for wheel installs: flake.nix+flake.lock+src/ copied via mkdtemp→rename swap; old tree renamed aside (mtime stamped to now) for the prune sweeper, never deleted inline (live podman binds pin inodes).

public_surface.name: mise config venv discovery (mise.toml/.mise.toml/mise.jail.toml/.mise.jail.toml)
public_surface.detail: Reads env._.python.venv (str, or dict.path default '.venv'); later file wins in that fixed order; strips leading '{{config_root}}/'; feeds the venv shadow set (.venv ∪ mise venv ∪ per_side_paths) and jail-made-venv retirement (pyvenv.cfg 'home =' pointing at /workspace/, /mise/, or host ~/.local/share/mise and nonexistent → rmtree).

public_surface.name: MISE_STORE_VOLUME='yolo-mise-data-v2'
public_surface.detail: Named volume for /mise on macOS (both podman and AC). Versioned name is a contract — renaming orphans user state.

internal_deps: cli/macos_user.py (lazy import for macos-user runtime dispatch)

third_party_deps.used_for: CLI command/option parsing, ctx.args pass-through, typer.Exit
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: cobra or urfave/cli (must replicate allow_extra_args/ignore_unknown_options `--` semantics)

third_party_deps.used_for: console.print markup ([bold red] etc.), stderr Console for profile output
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: fatih/color / charmbracelet/lipgloss; markup strings become the parity-sensitive part only if tests assert on them
tailscale/hujson or tidwall/jsonc (JSON5 leniency differences are a hazard)

side_effects: spawns podman/container/docker-CLI subprocesses (run, exec, ps, stop, rm, inspect, top, machine inspect, system status, ls)
creates/updates <workspace>/.yolo/home tree (dirs, touched files, seeded agent auth, migrations, deref copies)
deletes workspace venvs matching jail-made heuristic (_retire_jail_made_venv rmtree)
writes tracking file, owner-PID file, workspace flock file under ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/
spawns detached socat processes + appends to logs/<cname>-socat.log; rmtrees /tmp/yolo-fwd-<cname> on cleanup
starts/stops host-side loophole services + oauth-broker relay (via loopholes_runtime)
runs sys.executable -c entrypoint dry-run in a TemporaryDirectory (yolo-check-*)
stops orphaned containers (reaping) and removes stale ones
python_specifics: fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) on an open file object; lock lifetime tied to FD, released by close() from the tty-proxy on_started callback (possibly another thread) — Go: syscall.Flock with careful fd ownership
resource.getrlimit(RLIMIT_MEMLOCK) + RLIM_INFINITY sentinel → x/sys/unix.Getrlimit (Rlimit.Max uint64 ^0 semantics differ from Python's -1)
os.kill(pid, 0) tri-state liveness (ProcessLookupError/PermissionError/other-OSError) — Go: unix.Kill(pid,0) errno mapping must preserve fail-safe polarity
dynamic module reflection: sys.modules[__name__.rpartition('.')[0]].__file__ to locate cli/__init__.py — exists solely so tests can monkeypatch cli.__file__; Go needs an injectable path resolver
dual-try import of entrypoint.agent_registry under src.entrypoint vs entrypoint identities (in-jail vs host import path)

tests monkeypatch module-level names (cli.run_cmd.GLOBAL_MISE, LOCK_RELEASE_POLL_ATTEMPTS, SOCKET_WAIT_DEADLINE_SECONDS...) — the F401 re-imports in run_cmd exist only for this; Go port needs DI/config struct and test rewrite at the process boundary

shlex.join / shlex.quote exact quoting semantics — target_cmd is re-parsed by in-jail bash; Go has no stdlib shlex (use a vetted port, e.g. kballard/go-shellquote, and golden-test against Python output)
subprocess.Popen detached socat children inherited past process exit; log file FD deliberately leaked open
tempfile.TemporaryDirectory / mkdtemp + os.utime + copytree + atomic double-rename swap in _resolve_repo_root
tomllib for mise.toml, pyjson5 for JSONC — parser-leniency parity risk
typer ctx.args with allow_extra_args+ignore_unknown_options: unknown flags flow into the jail command untouched
sys.executable -c '<generated python>' preflight — Python-runs-Python; a Go CLI must either locate a Python interpreter + the repo src/ or call the (future) Go entrypoint's dry-run mode

in-place list surgery: run_cmd.index(image) to insert -e pairs before the image arg (ordering is a hard podman contract: flags before image); _inject_agent_yolo_flags mutates argv at index 1 in reverse
% -formatting with dict for the sh export template; '\''-style single-quote escaping
rich markup strings embedded in user-visible output

seams.name: container-runtime argv boundary (the big one)
seams.contract: run() is ultimately a pure-ish function: (workspace, config, env, host probes) → ordered podman/container argv + host-side FS mutations + service startups, then delegates execution to run_with_proxy(argv). YOLO_DEBUG=1 already prints the exact shlex-quoted argv.
seams.swap_mechanism: Replace the `yolo run` subcommand with a Go binary (or Python dispatches to `yolo-run-go` when YOLO_IMPL=go); everything downstream (image, entrypoint, tty proxy semantics) is unchanged because the interface is an argv exec.
seams.parity_test: Golden-compare YOLO_DEBUG argv output (Python vs Go) across a config matrix (agents, gpu, devices, ports, forward_host_ports, workspace_readonly, per_side_paths, ephemeral_storage, nested-jail env, macOS/Linux path stubs) with a fake `podman` shim on PATH recording argv; plus diff the ws_state tree and tracking/owner/lock files produced.

seams.name: runtime.py probe layer
seams.contract: Thin subprocess wrappers over podman/AC CLIs with fixed argv and parse rules (filters, --format templates, AC table parsing); pure inputs/outputs, no state beyond tracking files.
seams.swap_mechanism: Port first as a standalone Go package/binary; Python could shell out to `yolo-runtime-probe <verb>` returning JSON, or just port atomically with run — it has no cross-module state.
seams.parity_test: Record/replay: fake runtime CLI shims emitting canned podman/AC outputs; assert identical returns for find_running/existing, _live_yolo_containers (incl. None-vs-empty-set polarity), stuck heuristic, machine memory parse.

seams.name: network.py socat forwarder
seams.contract: Given (forward_host_ports list, cname, socket_dir): spawn `socat UNIX-LISTEN:<dir>/port-N.sock,fork,mode=777 TCP:127.0.0.1:M` per entry, log to logs/<cname>-socat.log, wait ≤2s for sockets; cleanup = terminate + rmtree.
seams.swap_mechanism: Smallest first-port candidate: a `yolo-fwd` Go helper (could even replace socat itself with native Go proxying) invoked by Python run(); or port with run_cmd. Process boundary already exists (Popen handles are opaque).
seams.parity_test: Start Python and Go versions against a listener on 127.0.0.1:N; connect through each unix socket, assert byte-for-byte tunnel + socket perms/paths + log file location + cleanup removes dir.

seams.name: entrypoint preflight subprocess
seams.contract: Spawn python -c <fixed code> with env {JAIL_HOME/HOME=tmp, MISE_DATA_DIR=/mise, YOLO_HOST_DIR, YOLO_BLOCK_CONFIG, YOLO_MISE_TOOLS, YOLO_LSP_SERVERS, YOLO_MCP_SERVERS, YOLO_MCP_PRESETS, YOLO_AGENTS, env_sources, PYTHONPATH dropped}; rc!=0 → ConfigError with combined stdout+stderr.
seams.swap_mechanism: Go keeps shelling to python3 -c against repo_root/src while entrypoint stays Python; when entrypoint is ported, switch to `<go-entrypoint> --dry-run` behind the same env contract.
seams.parity_test: Same env + same broken/valid configs → same pass/fail and same error text propagated.

seams.name: in-jail provisioning script string
seams.contract: The bash text of setup_script/provision_script/mise_activate/final_internal_cmd handed to `yolo-entrypoint` — consumed by in-jail bash, writes /workspace/.yolo/startup.log, honors YOLO_PROVISION_PROMPT/YOLO_STORE_PRUNE_OK.
seams.swap_mechanism: Keep the generated string byte-identical in Go (extract to shared golden fixtures); the jail side never knows which language generated it.
seams.parity_test: String-equality golden test of the generated script for profile/non-profile × prune-ok/not, plus a live nested-jail run comparing startup.log structure.

seams.name: host-services env + socket dir
seams.contract: -e YOLO_SERVICE_<NAME>_SOCKET=/run/yolo-services/<name>.sock inserted before image arg; host dir /tmp-based per-jail sockets dir mounted at /run/yolo-services.
seams.swap_mechanism: Owned by loopholes_runtime module group; run_cmd only consumes — port order-independent as long as env names/paths match.
seams.parity_test: In-jail `env | grep YOLO_SERVICE` + socket connect test identical under both CLIs.

existing: tests/test_cli_commands.py (4151 lines — heavy monkeypatch-based coverage of run() branches, mount assembly, env vars, flag injection, orphan reaping, preflight)
tests/test_cli_unit.py (5400 lines — helpers: _resolve_repo_root, _scratch_mount_args, _workspace_readonly_mount_args, _resolve_lsp_installs, port parsing, etc.)
tests/test_runtime.py (701 lines — runtime detection, naming, find/remove, AC table parsing)
tests/test_state_separation.py (760 lines — venv shadows, mise store, per-side paths, _retire_jail_made_venv)
gaps: no automated end-to-end container launch (repo convention: verify via nested jail manually — startup mount/permission errors only surface at real `podman run`)
signal-path teardown (_on_terminate, SIGHUP/SIGTERM) and lock-release-on-started timing largely untested
Apple Container branches only testable via parsing fakes, not real AC
GPU/KVM/USB device branches depend on host hardware — fake-probe coverage only
the generated bash script strings are asserted piecemeal, not as a full golden — Go port should add one
test_coverage.assessment: Unusually deep unit coverage (~12k test lines against ~3.8k module lines) but almost all of it is monkeypatch-at-module-attribute style, which does NOT transfer to Go — parity work should pivot to the process boundary: fake runtime shims on PATH + YOLO_DEBUG argv goldens + ws_state tree diffs, then a real nested-jail smoke run.

porting_hazards: Argv ORDER is a contract: all flags/env before the image arg, image then 'yolo-entrypoint' then the script; host-service -e pairs are inserted at index(image); mount ordering matters semantically (rw /workspace first, then :ro/shadow overlays that must shadow it). Golden tests must compare ordered argv, not sets.

shlex.quote/join parity: target_cmd round-trips through bash in-jail; a Go shellquote lib with different quoting (e.g. always-quote vs minimal) changes display_cmd escaping and could break the '⚡ Executing' printf and prompt-injection-safe quoting.
The embedded bash scripts must stay byte-identical (PIPESTATUS is bash-only in the outer shell; inner sh -c is POSIX; grep -v '^mise WARN' rc-preservation dance; ${VAR:-'default'} escaping).

Tests monkeypatch module globals (GLOBAL_MISE, LOCK_RELEASE_POLL_ATTEMPTS, SOCKET_WAIT_DEADLINE_SECONDS, cli.__file__) — the F401 keep-imports comment documents this; none of it ports. Plan a test rewrite, not a translation.

_resolve_repo_root's installed-wheel staging assumes flake.nix ships as package data next to src/ — a single Go binary has no package dir; must embed flake.nix+src or redefine step 3 while keeping steps 1/2/4 and the rename-aside (never-delete) invariant that protects live podman bind inodes.

_entrypoint_preflight requires a Python interpreter (sys.executable) and the repo's src/ on disk; Go port must pick an interpreter (python3 from PATH? bundled?) — behavior divergence risk in exotic envs.
None-vs-empty-set polarity in _live_yolo_containers (unknown ≠ nothing live) gates both YOLO_STORE_PRUNE_OK and relay reaping — easy to flatten to nil==empty in Go and silently grant unsafe prunes.
macOS /tmp→/private/tmp resolution (Path('/tmp').resolve()) affects socket-dir paths seen by Podman Machine; Go filepath.EvalSymlinks needed in the same spots only.

Apple Container divergences are scattered special cases (no :ro, no --filter/--format/top/-t-grace/--pids-limit/--cgroupns, ~22 mount limit, bare --tmpfs, --publish-socket, table parsing) — enumerate as a runtime-capability struct rather than re-scattering if-chains, but keep observable behavior identical incl. warning texts if tests assert them.
fcntl flock + FD lifetime + on_started callback releasing the lock from tty_proxy context — Go goroutine/fd ownership must not close early (releases lock) or leak (blocks concurrent yolo forever).
resource.RLIM_INFINITY (-1) vs Go's unix.RLIM_INFINITY (^uint64(0)) in the memlock --ulimit formatting ('memlock=-1:-1' literal).
pyjson5 vs Go JSONC parser leniency (unquoted keys, trailing commas, comments) for user config; tomllib vs BurntSushi/toml for mise config edge cases.
typer's `--` and ignore_unknown_options semantics: `yolo --profile -- claude --weird-flag` must split identically; agent flag injection at argv[1] with alias suppression (YOLO_FLAG_ALIASES).
Rich markup in output: if any test/doc asserts exact strings (warnings like the AC :ro skip message), the Go port needs matching plain-text renderings.

Time-based behaviors: 20×0.25s lock-release poll, 2s/5ms socket wait, 5s stop timeout, mtime-based nix-build-root staleness — keep constants and default values identical (tests shrink them by patching).
notes: This group is the integration hub: it imports from 13 sibling cli modules plus src/loopholes and the entrypoint agent registry, so it should be ported LAST among cli modules (or behind the argv seam with the siblings still Python via a JSON-over-exec bridge). The cleanest incremental strategy is bottom-up within the group: network.py first (149 lines, pure process boundary), runtime.py second (probe layer, replayable), run_cmd.py last with YOLO_DEBUG argv goldens as the primary parity harness plus a fake-podman PATH shim, finishing with a real nested-jail run (`yolo -- bash` from inside a jail) per the repo's testing convention. macos_user dispatch (runtime == 'macos-user') branches out of run() early and belongs to the macos module group. Registration surface lives in src/cli/__init__.py lines 354-378 (run/ps/doctor + the run command's allow_extra_args context settings).
